libraryDescription:
This library, gui.CheckBoxPanel, provides a panel containing arranged check boxes as a GUI component.

In the standard VCSSL library GUI, GUI components are handled as int IDs, but the GUI component provided by this module is handled as theCheckBoxPanel struct.

The CheckBoxPanel struct internally stores resource IDs and related data, but you normally do not need to be aware of its internals becausefunctions for creating and destroying it are provided. Direct access to its members is not recommended.
Specifically, create it with the newCheckBoxPanel function and destroy it with thedeleteComponent function.

This module also provides overloads of standard GUI library functions so that the CheckBoxPanel struct can be handled almost in the sameway as in the standard library GUI.
For example, the mountComponent, setComponentSize, andsetComponentLocation functions are provided.

function0.name: newCheckBoxPanel
function0.declaration: CheckBoxPanel newCheckBoxPanel(int x, int y, int width, int height, string labelText[ ], bool defaultValue[ ])
function0.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the panel. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the panel. 
(int type) width : The width of the panel. 
(int type) height : The height of the panel. 
(string[] type) labelText : The array storing the label text. 
defaultText : The array storing the initial values of the check boxes.
function0.returnValue: (CheckBoxPanel type) The created check box panel struct.

function1.name: deleteComponent
function1.parameters: (CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The check box panel to be destroyed.

function2.name: paintComponent
function2.parameters: (CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The check box panel to be repainted.

function3.name: mountComponent
function3.description: Mounts a check box panel onto another GUI component.
function3.parameters:
(CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The check box panel to be mounted. 
(int type) componentID : The destination GUI component ID.

function4.name: demountComponent
function4.description: Demounts a check box panel from another GUI component.
function4.parameters:
(CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The check box panel to be demounted. 
(int type) componentID : The destination GUI component ID.

function5.name: getPanel
function5.description: Returns the component ID of the panel.
function5.parameters: (CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel.
function5.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the panel.

function6.name: getCheckBox
function6.description: Gets the text field corresponding to the specified label.
function6.parameters:
(CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel. 
(string type) labelText : The label text corresponding to the check box to get.
function6.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the check box.

function7.name: getCheckBox
function7.description: Gets the text field at the specified index.
function7.parameters:
(CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel. 
(int type) index : The index of the check box.
function7.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the check box.

function8.name: getComponentSize
function8.description: Returns the size of the panel.
function8.parameters: (CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel.
function8.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the size of the panel.

function9.name: setComponentSize
function9.declaration: void setComponentSize(CheckBoxPanel &panelInstance, int width, int height)
function9.description: Sets the size of the panel.
function9.parameters:
(CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel. 
(int type) width : The width of the panel. 
(int type) height : The height of the panel.

function10.name: setComponentSize
function10.description: Automatically adjusts the sizes of the internal components to match the current panel size. Use this when the panel size has been changed externally.
function10.parameters: (CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel.

function11.name: getComponentLocation
function11.description: Returns the location of the panel.
function11.parameters: (CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel.
function11.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the location of the panel.

function12.name: setComponentLocation
function12.declaration: void setComponentLocation(CheckBoxPanel &panelInstance, int x, int y)
function12.description: Sets the location of the panel.
function12.parameters:
(CheckBoxPanel type) panelInstance : The target check box panel. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the window.
